completion.long:
To load completions:

Bash:

  $ source <(lilith completion bash)

  # To load completions for each session, execute once:
  # Linux:
  $ lilith completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/lilith
  # macOS:
  $ lilith completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/lilith

Zsh:

  # If shell completion is not already enabled in your environment,
  # you will need to enable it. You can execute the following once:

  $ echo "autoload -U compinit; compinit" >> ~/.zshrc

  # To load completions for each session, execute once:
  $ lilith completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_lilith"

  # You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

fish:

  $ lilith completion fish | source

  # To load completions for each session, execute once:
  $ lilith completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/lilith.fish

PowerShell:

  PS> lilith completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

  # To load completions for every new session, run:
  PS> lilith completion powershell > lilith.ps1
  # and source this file from your PowerShell profile.

root.long: Interpret hotkeys into markdown keyboard visuals

display.long: Scan EDN metadata & output Markdown table

interpret.short: Generate program‐specific configs from EDN annotations

embed.short: Insert program-specific configs from EDN annotations
